commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: A narrow asset cleanup is needed, but the draft also changes Gateway session-history semantics and should stay with human maintainer review rather than an autonomous repair lane.; Needs attention: The WebChat pagination code did not show a direct security regression, but the unrelated generated browser-runtime asset rewrite needs provenance before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Drop the unrelated generated runtime rew... (sha=4b4b4a212235545b973ed23039822fd0c702d788)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds cursor-based Gateway `chat.history` pagination and WebChat older-message loading, updates docs/tests/mock scenarios, and also changes the generated diffs viewer runtime asset.

PR surface: Source +17573, Tests +501, Docs +2, Other +102. Total +18178 across 68 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main validates `chat.history` without `beforeSeq` and WebChat only replaces the current loaded history, so older-page recovery is not available on main. I did not run a live repro because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow asset cleanup is needed, but the draft also changes Gateway session-history semantics and should stay with human maintainer review rather than an autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(webchat): paginate tool-heavy chat history
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- WebChat history could bury useful user/assistant turns when tool-heavy transcript entries consumed bounded history windows.
- During review, this branch also needed merge-conflict resolution with `origin/main` in `chat.history` logic.

Why does this matter now?

- Users need a reliable recovery path to older conversational context in active sessions.
- The PR must stay mergeable and preserve both pagination behavior and newer mainline history-filter behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- `chat.history` supports cursor-based pagination (`beforeSeq`, `hasMore`, `nextBeforeSeq`, `oldestSeq`, `newestSeq`).
- WebChat can load older messages incrementally via `Load older messages`.
- Merge resolution keeps pagination intact while preserving upstream stale pre-session announce filtering behavior.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Broad transport/provider matrix validation (Crabbox/Testbox full gates).
- Unrelated refactors outside chat history pagination and merge-conflict reconciliation.

What does success look like?

- Recent history loads immediately.
- Older pages prepend correctly, dedupe correctly, and stop when `hasMore=false`.
- Gateway history behavior remains correct for hidden-tail and stale-announce boundary cases after merging `origin/main`.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` merge resolution correctness around `readProjectedChatHistoryPageAsync`.
- Cursor metadata correctness under byte-budget and filtered-history conditions.
- WebChat prepend/dedupe behavior with paged loads.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- Related # (not provided in this draft)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Yes. Maintainer feedback requested resolving merge conflicts on this PR.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist. Maintainer context helps reviewers and automation distinguish intended work from drive-by churn.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: WebChat paged history recovery for tool-heavy transcripts, plus conflict-resolved compatibility with mainline stale pre-session announce filtering.
- Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree and Control UI mock dev server at `http://127.0.0.1:5187/chat`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git fetch --unshallow origin`
  - `git fetch origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main`
  - `git merge origin/main`
  - `corepack enable && corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Targeted suite passed: 7 files, 362 tests.
  - Conflict resolved and merged in commit `4b4b4a2122`.
- Observed result after fix:
  - Pagination behavior remains functional and bounded.
  - Older-message loading works as expected.
  - Boundary/hidden-tail history tests and stale-announce overread tests pass after merge resolution.
- What was not tested:
  - Full Crabbox/Testbox broad-gate validation.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints:
  - `parallel_validation` timed out in this environment.
  - No external cloud-provider validation performed in this pass.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  - Prior to conflict resolution, merge failed with conflict in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` and targeted history tests failed until reconciliation.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes. Terminal screenshots, copied live output, redacted runtime logs, recordings, and linked artifacts count.

If your environment cannot produce the ideal proof, explain that under `Proof limitations or environment constraints` so reviewers and ClawSweeper can direct the next step properly.

Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `corepack enable && corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Existing gateway regression coverage for pagination, hidden-tail backfill, and stale announce boundary filtering was re-validated against the merged code path.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Merge conflict in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.
- Targeted gateway history tests failed before final conflict reconciliation.

If no test was added, why not?

- No new behavior was introduced beyond merge reconciliation; existing targeted tests already covered the affected runtime paths and now pass.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check. CI is useful support, but external PRs still need real behavior proof above when behavior changes.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes (original pagination fix), with merge resolution preserving intended behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Gateway `chat.history` filtering/pagination interaction in `readProjectedChatHistoryPageAsync`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Targeted gateway + UI test suites were run after conflict resolution, including edge-case tests for hidden tails and stale announce boundaries.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff. ClawSweeper can see touched files, but it cannot know which behavior you think is risky, why the risk is acceptable, or what mitigation reviewers should verify.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review of the merge-resolution commit and CI confirmation.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI/maintainer final review; optional broader environment proof if requested.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed maintainer request to resolve merge conflicts (`@BunsDev`), completed in commit `4b4b4a2122`.

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress. If useful information appears in comments, fold the current next action or blocker back here so maintainers and ClawSweeper do not need to reconstruct state from comment history.

</details>
target.author: bunsdev
